research_tasks.task_title: Replot TDTR cooling curves for r-MoS2 layer-number series
research_tasks.task_description: Using the provided Excel workbook, reproduce the TDTR time-domain thermoreflectance figure showing cooling curves for r-MoS2 films with different layer numbers. Identify the sheet(s) containing time-delay traces and model-fit curves, then generate a publication-style plot of normalized thermoreflectance signal versus pump-probe delay time. Preserve the multi-series structure for the different layer numbers (including the experimental traces and fitted/model curves), use clear legends, and match the figure’s scientific presentation conventions as closely as possible. If the workbook contains multiple subpanels or related TDTR traces, keep them together in a single figure layout consistent with the source figure.
execution_steps: Step_0: Open the workbook and identify the sheet(s) containing TDTR time-delay data, including both experimental and fitted/model columns.
Step_1: Parse the independent variable as time delay (ps) and the dependent variable as the TDTR response signal (typically -V_in/V_out or equivalent).
Step_2: Group the traces by layer number N and distinguish experimental data from model-fit curves using column names or paired series labels.
Step_3: Plot all TDTR traces on the same axes with appropriate line styles, markers, and legend entries for N = 1, 2, and 10 (or any additional series present).
Step_4: Format the figure with labeled axes, units, and a clean scientific style suitable for direct comparison to the paper’s TDTR panel.

ground_truth_outcome.description: A replot of the TDTR cooling curves showing multiple layer-number-dependent traces with corresponding model fits, suitable to reproduce the paper’s TDTR transport panel.

research_tasks.task_title: Replot thermal resistance versus layer number for r-MoS2 and r-WS2
research_tasks.task_description: Using the provided Excel workbook, reproduce the figure that plots TDTR-derived thermal boundary resistance as a function of layer number for r-MoS2 and r-WS2. Locate the sheet(s) containing thermal resistance values, layer number N, and any fitted trend lines or extracted parameters. Create a publication-quality plot with the two material series clearly distinguished, and include the linear or model fits used to extract the through-plane thermal transport trend. If uncertainties or error bars are present, display them. The goal is to reconstruct the figure panel that compares thermal resistance scaling with thickness/layer number across the two materials.
execution_steps: Step_0: Open the workbook and identify the sheet(s) containing thermal resistance data versus layer number, including any fit parameters or uncertainty columns.
Step_1: Extract the independent variable N and the dependent variable thermal resistance R_TDTR (m^2 K/GW) or equivalent.
Step_2: Separate the r-MoS2 and r-WS2 series and determine whether the table includes fitted values for N > 1 or a linear trend used in the analysis.
Step_3: Plot thermal resistance versus layer number with distinct symbols/colors for each material and overlay the fit curves if available.
Step_4: Add axis labels, units, legend, and error bars where applicable, then format the plot to match a paper-ready transport figure.

ground_truth_outcome.description: A replot of thermal resistance versus layer number for both r-MoS2 and r-WS2, including fitted trends used to analyze through-plane transport.

research_tasks.task_title: Replot temperature-dependent thermal conductivity from experiment and MD
research_tasks.task_description: Using the provided Excel workbook, reproduce the temperature-dependence figure comparing thermal conductivity from experiment and molecular dynamics for bulk MoS2 and r-MoS2. Identify the sheet(s) containing temperature, in-plane thermal conductivity, out-of-plane thermal conductivity, and any experimental versus simulation series. Generate a multi-series line plot with temperature on the x-axis and thermal conductivity on the y-axis, preserving the distinction between in-plane and out-of-plane components and between bulk MoS2 and r-MoS2. If the workbook includes both experimental and MD results, plot them together with clear legend entries and consistent styling so the anisotropy and temperature trends can be visually compared.
execution_steps: Step_0: Open the workbook and locate the sheet(s) containing thermal conductivity versus temperature data.
Step_1: Extract temperature (K) as the independent variable and the conductivity columns for in-plane and out-of-plane transport.
Step_2: Identify which series correspond to bulk MoS2, r-MoS2, experimental values, and MD simulation outputs.
Step_3: Plot conductivity versus temperature with separate curves for each transport direction and material, using a legend that clearly distinguishes all series.
Step_4: Format the axes, units, and line styles to produce a clean figure that reproduces the paper’s thermal anisotropy comparison panel.

ground_truth_outcome.description: A replot of thermal conductivity versus temperature showing in-plane and out-of-plane trends for bulk MoS2 and r-MoS2, with experimental and MD series distinguished where present.
